completedWork: Resonant Circuit Bandwidth Simulation - NEW: Interactive RLC circuit bandwidth and Q factor exploration tool. Features: Series/parallel RLC circuit configurations, adjustable components (inductance: 0.1-1000H/mH/μH, capacitance: 0.1-10000F/μF/nF/pF, resistance: 1-10000Ω), real-time calculations for resonant frequency (f₀ = 1/2π√LC), quality factor (Q = ω₀L/R for series, Q = R/ω₀L for parallel), bandwidth (BW = f₀/Q), -3dB cutoff frequencies (f₁ and f₂). Visual feedback: Interactive frequency response curve with animated plotting, color-coded markers for peak (green), -3dB points (orange), and bandwidth indication (purple dashed line), Q factor interpretation panel with selectivity bar, circuit type behavior indicators. Educational content: Bandwidth and Q factor theory, -3dB point explanations, practical applications (radio tuning, bandpass filters, oscillators, audio systems), interactive learning tips for exploring circuit parameters.

Inductor Magnetic Field Calculator - NEW: Solenoid magnetic field strength calculation tool. Features: Core type selection (air core μr=1, ferrite core with adjustable μr: 1-10000), component inputs (number of turns: 1-10000, current: 0-10A/mA, solenoid length: 0.1-100m/cm/mm, diameter: 0.1-100m/cm/mm), preset core materials (iron powder μr=100, ferrite μr=1000, high-μ ferrite μr=2000, nanocrystalline μr=5000), real-time calculations for magnetic field B = μ₀μr×N×I/L (center of solenoid), ampere-turns (NI), turn density (n = N/L). Visual feedback: Interactive solenoid diagram with animated magnetic field lines, field direction arrows, core type visualization, current indicator, dimension labels (length L and diameter D). Educational content: Solenoid theory and formulas, core material comparisons (air core vs ferrite core), magnetic field formulas (inside solenoid, at center point, on axis), common applications (electromagnets, relays, inductors, transformers, motors, sensors), design considerations (ampere-turns, saturation, temperature, frequency), typical field strength comparison table (Earth's field ~50μT, refrigerator magnet ~10mT, MRI ~1.5T, research magnets up to 45T), field strength scale visualization with comparison markers, design tips (increasing field strength, saturation warnings, power considerations).
